description:
## Model Component Registration

Register a dynamically simulated model component.

Each component type (atmosphere, ocean, etc.) in a top-level model requires separate registration.

See the [EMD Specification](https://wcrp-cmip.github.io/Essential-Model-Documentation/) for detailed guidance.

label: Component Name
description:
The name of the model component.

If embedded with no common name, use format: [HostName]-[component]

Example: HadAM3-aerosol

label: Component Family
description: The component's family name (if applicable).\n\n<collapse>A family of models share much of their code bases, but may be configured in different ways (e.g. different resolutions, parameter choices, or the inclusion or not of particular physical process). See, for instance, [Masson and Knutti (2011)](https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1029/2011GL046864) for an example of how the family can be used to inform model genealogies.</collapse>

label: Scientific Description
description: Scientific overview of the component.\n\nSummarise the key processes simulated.
placeholder: Describe the key processes simulated by this component...

description:
## Component Relationships

A component is either **embedded in** a host OR **coupled with** other components, never both.

**Embedded**: Tightly integrated, shares host's grid, hard to extract
**Coupled**: Recognizable name/version, exchanges through coupler, could transfer to another model

label: Code Base URL
description:
The location of the source code for the model component.

The location must be a fully URL, and could be a unique persistent identifier (DOI), and should prefereably point to a specific version/commit.

Enter 'private' if not publicly available.

label: Reference DOIs
description: Enter DOI URLs to published work for the model component, one per line.

label: Issue Kind
description: Select whether this is a new submission or modification of existing entry.
